dotfiles reports baseline quality coverage

how agent quality is measured

This repo treats its own AI agent like production software: a test pyramid, a regression gate, and scored end-to-end runs. Here is the system that keeps the agent honest, distilled from the design notes in issue #70.

Why it exists

The agent's behaviour degraded over a two-week window. Investigation found two compounding causes: an upstream adaptive-thinking regression that cut reasoning depth, and a near-doubling of the instruction surface during the same window. The fix was not "write better rules" - it was to measure compliance continuously, so regressions surface immediately instead of weeks later.

The testing pyramid

Tier 3 - E2E
Real interactive tmux sessions, real claude, hooks firing. 6 scenarios scored 0-100 (NPS) with penalties.
Tier 2 - Integration
claude -p with the real CLAUDE.md, stream-json tool-call parsing, workspace artifact checks. 7 scenarios.
Tier 1 - Static evals
Prompt evals on haiku across compliance, routing, navigation and knowledge. This is the baseline pass rate, gated in CI and pre-push.
Tier 0 - Quick
bats, pytest, QML, frontmatter. No model calls, runs in under ten seconds.

NPS scoring

Tiers 2 and 3 do not just count pass or fail - they score each run 0-100 with explicit penalties, so a technically-passing but sloppy run cannot hide. Earlier scoring was bonus-only and inflated results; penalties were added to make the number trustworthy.

What the experiments found

An A/B harness compared how instructions are loaded into the agent:

loading methodscorefinding
@reference in CLAUDE.md83best - complements the harness built-ins
no instructions78model defaults are already decent
inline76good
--system-prompt67worst - competes with harness built-ins

Key takeaway: given enough thinking time the agent scored a perfect 100 on the workflow scenario, which means the instructions themselves are sound - the failures were model capacity gaps, not instruction gaps.

Prompt-engineering strategy

XML-tagged rule boundaries zero-ambiguity constraints override block: custom > default ~5 lines per rule, dense PostCompact re-injects top rules @reference loading
Distilled from issue #70 · see the live baseline dashboard
